notes.note: The letter-heading. The recipient is addressed only by the cognomen Rex; the identification is uncertain, though he is likely Q. Marcius Rex. The bare cognomen is all the heading supplies.

Hospitium (guest-friendship) was a hereditary, reciprocal bond of hospitality and protection between families of different communities, here between Cicero and a Maltese household. To call Aristoteles his hospes antiquissimus is to claim the strongest non-kin obligation Roman custom recognised.

notes.anchor: I obtained his pardon from Caesar
notes.note: After the civil war Caesar exercised personal clementia, granting pardon to former Pompeians; Cicero, restored to favour, frequently brokered such pardons. Aristoteles had taken the senatorial side even longer than Cicero, which made the intercession the more delicate.
